title: Kotlin Data Classes
content:
### Data Classes in Kotlin

Data classes are a concise way to create classes that primarily hold data. The compiler automatically generates `equals()`, `hashCode()`, `toString()`, `copy()`, and `componentN()` functions.

---

### Declaring Data Classes

```kotlin
data class User(val name: String, val age: Int)
```

---

### Auto-Generated Functions

```kotlin
data class Person(val name: String, val age: Int)

fun main() {
    val p1 = Person("Alice", 30)
    val p2 = Person("Alice", 30)
    
    println(p1)                // Person(name=Alice, age=30)
    println(p1 == p2)          // true (structural equality)
    println(p1.hashCode())     // Consistent with equals
}
```

---

### The copy() Function

```kotlin
data class Person(val name: String, val age: Int)

fun main() {
    val alice = Person("Alice", 30)
    val olderAlice = alice.copy(age = 31)
    
    println(alice)        // Person(name=Alice, age=30)
    println(olderAlice)   // Person(name=Alice, age=31)
}
```

---

### Destructuring Declarations

Data classes support destructuring via `componentN()` functions.

```kotlin
data class Point(val x: Int, val y: Int)

fun main() {
    val point = Point(10, 20)
    val (x, y) = point  // Destructuring
    
    println("x=$x, y=$y")  // x=10, y=20
}
```

---

### Data Class Requirements

| Requirement | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| Primary constructor parameters | Must have at least one parameter with `val` or `var` |
| No `open`, `abstract`, `sealed`, `inner` | Data classes are final |
| Extending classes | Cannot extend other classes (but can implement interfaces) |

---

### Data Classes with Default Values

```kotlin
data class Config(
    val host: String = "localhost",
    val port: Int = 8080,
    val ssl: Boolean = false
)

fun main() {
    val config = Config(host = "example.com")
    val default = Config()
    
    println(config)   // Config(host=example.com, port=8080, ssl=false)
    println(default)   // Config(host=localhost, port=8080, ssl=false)
}
```

> **Key Point:** Data classes are ideal for value objects, DTOs, and model classes. They eliminate boilerplate for `equals`, `hashCode`, `toString`, and more.
codeExample:
data class Product(
    val id: Int,
    val name: String,
    val price: Double,
    val inStock: Boolean = true
)

fun main() {
    val p1 = Product(1, "Laptop", 999.99)
    val p2 = p1.copy(price = 899.99)
    
    println(p1)
    println(p2)
    println("Same product: ${p1 == p2}")
    
    val (id, name, price) = p1
    println("$id: $name costs \$$price")
}

quiz.question: Which function is NOT automatically generated for data classes?
Can a data class extend another class?
